According to van Heekeren (1958:77), the 'Cabengian' flake industry of Sulawesi consists of small- to medium-sized flakes, tiake tools (i.e. 'scrapers', 'points') and blades made from a variety of siliceous sedimentary rocks (see also Bailstra 1978c; Bartstra el al. 1991 | e also Bailstra 1978c; Bartstra el al. 1991, 1994; Keates 2004; Keates and Bartstra 1994, 2001; Sartono 1979). Although uncertain about their precise age, van Heekeren (1958) assigned a Late Pleistocene antiquity to the Cabengian on typological grounds.
